threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep asking about bus routes instead of just checking Google Maps like everyone else. It’s like you’re stuck in 2010!
Caught a guy serenading the night bus crew with an off-key version of 'Wonderwall'. Only in Brixton, where every ride feels like a gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I lived here? Sitting in the Kottbusser Tor U-Bahn, sharing a bench with a punk, a tourist, and a grandma all at once.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will a guy offer you a kebab while casually discussing Goethe’s poetry. It's like a street corner lecture over late-night snacks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name all the taquerias and still argue about which has the best lengua. The tourists just stick to Chipotle.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 19 Polk playing a sax while the driver was stuck in traffic. Only in the Tenderloin do you get a free concert during rush hour.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just Gaudí and parks, but it's the little art shops and local cafés that pulse with the real vibe of the barrio.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new when they still gasp at the €1.50 price of a café con leche at a bar, thinking it's a steal rather than the norm.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus doesn’t stop at a designated ‘bus stop’ here. Welcome to Dalston; just wave like you mean it.
Last night, the 36 bus was packed with a group singing sea shanties. Didn't know Peckham had a maritime mood!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stumbled upon a street artist painting murals while a busker serenaded an old couple dancing in the park. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a Döner at 3 AM and hear live techno blasting from a hidden backyard party. It's like a late-night Berlin rite of passage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know that the best tacos come from the unmarked truck on 24th and Harrison, while newcomers flock to the shiny spots on Valencia.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 31 Balboa this morning while chatting about his tax returns. Only in the Tenderloin do you get such multitasking.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí but miss the local bars where we sip on homemade vermut while the sun sets over Plaça del Sol.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still looking up at the street signs, squinting at the maps while bumping into locals on their phones.
